Daniel Christopher Morales

artifacts.content:
From: leasingoffice@monroestreetflats.com
To: daniel.morales83@gmail.com
Date: 2026-02-19 09:14
Subject: Clarification Needed – Intended Move-Out Date

Hi Daniel,

Thanks for submitting your online notice to vacate for Apt 3B at 314 West Monroe.

I see that you selected **March 31, 2026** as your anticipated move-out date on the form, but under “additional comments” you mentioned that your new lease may not begin until mid-April, depending on when the unit on the west side is ready. Before we finalize anything in our system, we need you to confirm the exact date you intend to surrender keys so we can coordinate turnover with maintenance and cleaning.

As a reminder, your current lease term runs through **May 31, 2026**, so choosing a move-out date earlier than that will prorate your final month’s rent. Also, because you’ve been here over two and a half years, we’re waiving the standard re-paint fee provided there’s no excessive damage.

You also mentioned in your comments that the constant late-night arguments from 3A have been triggering your anxiety and that, after your panic attack on June 3, 2024, your therapist (Dr. Alicia Bennett at Memorial Behavioral Health) encouraged you to look for a calmer environment. I’m sorry to hear it’s been so stressful. While we will continue to address noise complaints, we understand your decision to move.

Please reply with your **intended move-out date** by Friday, 2/23, so we can update your file and send the official Move-Out Checklist.

Best,

Carla Jennings
Property Manager, Monroe Street Flats

From: moveouts@monroestreetflats.com
To: daniel.morales83@gmail.com
Date: 2026-03-05 14:02
Subject: Move-Out Inspection Options for Apt 3B

Hi Daniel,

We received your preliminary notice that you plan to vacate Apt 3B sometime in **late March or early April 2026**, but we do not yet have a confirmed move-out date on file.

To schedule your walk-through inspection, please choose one of the following tentative dates and times and reply to this email with your preference:

- Thursday, **March 26, 2026** – 4:30 p.m.
- Saturday, **March 28, 2026** – 10:00 a.m.
- Tuesday, **April 7, 2026** – 5:15 p.m.

The inspection typically takes 20–30 minutes. You’ll need to have most of your belongings removed so we can accurately assess any damage beyond normal wear and tear.

You noted in your online form that noise from neighboring units has been affecting your ability to work from home and contributing to increased anxiety. We’re sorry that your experience at Monroe Street Flats hasn’t been as peaceful as you’d hoped. While we’ll continue to address behavior issues with residents in 3A, we understand that you’re seeking a quieter environment.

Once you confirm the inspection date, we’ll email your Final Account Statement estimate, including prorated rent based on your **actual move-out date**, plus any remaining utility charges.

Thank you for being a resident with us since 2023. Please let us know if you need clarification on the move-out process or prorations.

Regards,

Darryl King
Move-Out Coordinator
Monroe Street Flats

Prairie Ridge Flats

1189 Westchester Drive

artifacts.content:
From: Marissa Morales <marissa.morales.edu@gmail.com>
To: Daniel Morales <daniel.morales@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-02-02 21:17
Subject: That townhouse you sent me

Hey Danny,

I finally had a chance to look at the rental listing you texted earlier. The place on Birchwood actually looks really nice. If you don’t take it, I might steal it for myself when my lease is up.

The photos of the living room and the little patio are great, and it seems way quieter than your current downtown spot. I like that it’s still close enough to Mom and Dad but not right on top of them.

Just to make sure I’ve got it right, the address was 1842 Birchwood Lane, Springfield, IL 62703, right? You mentioned it’s a 2BR, 1.5 bath, and the rent was listed at $1,375? That seems like a steal compared to what you’re paying at 314 West Monroe Street, Apt 3B.

If you end up applying, make sure you double‑check that the landlord has the correct unit number on the lease. Remember the mess I had last year when my Decatur landlord mis‑typed my apartment number and my mail kept getting sent to 1904B instead of 1904A.

Also, I know you’re stressed about the timing with PrairieLine cutting your hours and all that, but honestly I think getting out of your current building will help your sleep and your blood pressure.

Call me tomorrow night and we can run through pros/cons together.

Love you,
Marissa

I also still have my old work desk line written down in a few places from before we went mostly hybrid (that one was 217-555-4410), but it just forwards to a general office voicemail now, so I’d rather not use it on anything important.

If the form asks for an alternate number, I can put Sophie’s cell since she’ll probably be co-signing if we go that route, but I’d rather keep everything going through my personal phone so I don’t miss calls about the background check or move-in dates.

Also, on the ID section, if for some reason my driver’s license doesn’t scan correctly when I upload it, I do have a backup: the temporary Cook County court-issued photo badge they made me wear during my jury duty service at the Daley Center in Chicago from March 6–9, 2023, when I was on a panel for a drug possession case. Let me know if that would be acceptable as a secondary form of ID.

Please confirm that listing 217-555-9726 as my main contact number is okay.

Best,
Daniel

Need to call the tenant screening/background check company that Oakview Terraces uses. They left a voicemail on the old PrairieLine landline (217-555-4410) asking me to verify details from my rental application, but that number just dumps into the general office mailbox now.

Action items during call:

1. Confirm they have my correct personal cell as the main contact for the “applicant details > phone number” field. They might still be trying the outdated work number or even the ancient 217-555-6043 I used back when I first moved to Springfield.

2. Ask them to update their records to show only my current mobile and remove any references to the office line or my parents’ house.

3. Verify they received the scanned copy of my driver’s license. If they had trouble reading it, offer to resend a clearer image. Mention that, if absolutely necessary, I could technically use the temporary Cook County court-issued photo badge they made me wear during my jury duty service at the Daley Center in Chicago from March 6–9, 2023, when I was on a panel for a drug possession case, as a backup ID.

Goal: Make sure all future calls and texts about the apartment come straight to my current cell so nothing falls through the cracks.

For day-to-day stuff, I’m easiest to reach on my personal cell, which is currently **(217) 555-4921**. Description:

Agenda:
- Follow up on prior written complaints (May & July 2024)
- Discuss ongoing marijuana smoke and chemical odors from Apt 2D and back stairwell
- Clarify next steps and options for early lease termination

Notes:
I scheduled this call after yet another weekend of strong marijuana smoke and what honestly smelled like meth coming up through the stairwell vents. The headaches are getting more frequent, and I’m done trying to sleep with a towel shoved under the door.

I’ll be calling Lisa Carter, the property manager, from my cell at **(217) 555-9026** so she recognizes the number. She usually calls back from the building office line, **(217) 555-6640**, or sometimes from her personal cell, which I think ends in 44 but I don’t have it saved.

Key points to raise:
- I’m leaving this address because the constant marijuana smoke and occasional meth smell from Apt 2D and the back stairwell have been giving me headaches.
- I already filed two written complaints with Lisa in May and July 2024 with no real follow-up.
- I’d rather move than keep living in a building where drug use is ignored.

If she asks for updated contact info for future landlords, I’ll confirm my current mobile and let her know email is still best for anything formal.
